_meta.notes: Master catalog of discovered roles. The daily scan APPENDS new postings here and never deletes, so history is preserved. Per-job Applied/Stop-tracking status lives in the dashboard (browser), keyed by job id, so it survives every regeneration. The 'fit' block is computed against Taylor's resume (see config.json.resume).

jobs.title: Studio QA Director
jobs.notes: Reports to VP Product Dev. Live-service multiplayer, cross-platform PC/console/mobile. Owns QA budget & vendor partnerships.

fit.why: Director-level, live-service multiplayer, owns QA budget + vendor partnerships — a near-exact match to your Fortis/Amazon scope.
fit.blurb: As Director of QA & Localization at Fortis Games, I built a studio-wide QA discipline and automation roadmap for live-service titles from the ground up, and I'm now leading Fortis' AI transformation of QA — using agentic AI and LLMs to target an 80% cut in manual release-test time. That blend of cross-platform live-service ownership and modern AI-driven automation is exactly what scaling competitive titles demands.

jobs.title: Senior QA Manager (Fortnite)
jobs.notes: Leads internal + external leads/managers/ICs across Fortnite in-development projects.

fit.why: Leading internal + external leads/managers on a flagship live-service title is squarely your wheelhouse; slightly below your current Director title.
fit.blurb: Leading blended internal and vendor QA teams on a flagship live-service title mirrors my work at Amazon Games and Fortis, where I now drive an AI-led automation transformation to slash manual release testing. At Blizzard I owned test operations for Overwatch and Battle.net, so the scale and live-ops cadence of Fortnite are familiar territory.

jobs.title: QA Manager (Online team)
jobs.notes: QA Manager for Epic's Online services team.

fit.why: Online-services QA is relevant (Battle.net, live ops), but a Manager title is under-leveled vs your Director background.
fit.blurb: Online-services quality is core to my background — I led test operations for Battle.net at Blizzard and online/live-service pipelines at Amazon and Fortis. I'd bring director-level rigor in CI/CD, telemetry, and incident response to Epic's Online team.

jobs.title: Director, QA - Unpublished R&D Product
jobs.notes: Owns QA process for a large team. Requires 10+ yrs QA, 3+ yrs leadership.

fit.why: Director owning QA for a greenfield R&D product; you exceed the 10+ yrs / 3+ leadership bar, bring AI-driven test automation, and LA is your home region.
fit.blurb: I've owned QA process and people for large game organizations for over a decade — at Fortis, Netflix, Amazon Games, and Blizzard — and I'm currently leading an AI transformation of QA using agentic tooling and LLMs. Standing up quality and modern AI-assisted automation for an unannounced R&D product is exactly the greenfield work I do today at Fortis, and I'm based in your Los Angeles region.

jobs.title: Senior QA Project Manager, Games
jobs.notes: QA program/project management for Netflix Games. Manager-track (verify scope vs people-management).

fit.why: You were Head of Publishing QA at Netflix and ran QA program management at Amazon — insider knowledge of Netflix QA process is a major edge.
fit.blurb: I previously served as Head of Publishing QA at Netflix, where I built the 3P Publishing QA strategy and JIRA/process standards as the portfolio scaled toward 95+ titles — so I'd ramp on Netflix Games QA program management on day one. My Amazon Games tenure as Program Manager for QA Automation & Tools adds the cross-team delivery and roadmap experience this role needs.

jobs.title: QA Director (Monopoly GO!)
jobs.notes: Surfaced via Glassdoor remote QA Director search. Confirm exact posting on next scan.

fit.why: Mobile live-service Director role — matches your Fortis mobile/live-service QA leadership and remote preference.
fit.blurb: Directing QA for a top-grossing mobile live-service title aligns directly with my work at Fortis Games, where I built the mobile-first QA discipline and now lead its AI transformation — leveraging agentic AI to target an 80% cut in manual release-test time. I pair that with deep LiveOps incident management honed across Blizzard, Amazon, and Netflix.

jobs.title: Director of QA
jobs.notes: Fintech/SaaS transformation. Owns QA systems & processes for product lines.

fit.why: Director-level software QA leadership transfers cleanly, and DFIN's modernization mandate is a direct match for the AI-driven test-automation transformation you lead at Fortis.
fit.blurb: Modernizing QA systems and processes across product lines is exactly what I do — at Fortis I stood up CI pipelines and a centralized framework and now lead an AI transformation using LLMs and agentic tooling to target an 80% reduction in manual release testing. That automation-and-AI playbook translates directly to DFIN's SaaS transformation, even coming from a games background.

jobs.title: VP of Quality
jobs.notes: Strategic + operational quality executive. Non-games. Confirm direct posting next scan.

fit.why: VP title is a great trajectory step, but a pharmacy 'VP of Quality' is likely regulatory/operational quality (GMP), not software QA — verify before investing.
fit.blurb: My two decades of executive quality leadership — defining standards, performance targets, and incident-response programs across global teams — would translate to a VP of Quality mandate, though I'd want to confirm the role centers on software/product quality rather than pharmaceutical regulatory compliance.

jobs.title: QA Associate Director
jobs.notes: Hybrid Barcelona. Reports to Head of QA; oversees QA Managers and Department Leads across the QA Business Unit. Mobile game studio.

fit.why: Games QA leadership with clear people-management scope, but Associate Director reports into the Head of QA (below your current Director level) and requires relocation to Barcelona.
fit.blurb: Managing QA leaders and driving quality strategy across Gameloft's global mobile portfolio aligns with my experience building cross-platform QA organizations at Fortis, Netflix, and Amazon. I'd bring a director-level playbook — including my current AI-driven automation initiative targeting an 80% cut in manual release-test time — to help scale Gameloft's QA Business Unit.

jobs.title: QA Manager, Apps
jobs.notes: Leads 6-8 FT QA Coordinators + outsourced testers for Roblox Player App (70M+ DAU). Platform Orchestration team. Weekly mobile release cadence. Exceptional comp.

fit.why: Leading QA for a 70M-DAU gaming platform at exceptional comp, but QA Manager is under-leveled vs your Director background and role is onsite San Mateo.
fit.blurb: Leading QA for Roblox's Player App — enjoyed by 70+ million daily users — is the high-stakes mobile release environment I've been building toward at Fortis and Amazon Games. I'd bring director-level rigor in release-cycle quality, CI/CD automation strategy, and people development to a team operating at Roblox's unprecedented scale.

jobs.title: Director, Quality Engineering
jobs.notes: Leads enterprise-wide QA strategy across QA engineers, SDETs, automation engineers, and accessibility/compliance testers. SaaS health-tech platform. EST/CST timezone preferred. 10+ yrs exp, 4+ yrs leading QA orgs.

fit.why: Director-level QE leadership with automation modernization mandate is a strong skills match — even across the games-to-health-tech domain gap — and fully remote.
fit.blurb: Standardizing QA processes and driving test automation enterprise-wide is exactly the transformation I lead at Fortis today — where I use LLMs and agentic AI to target an 80% reduction in manual release testing. I'd apply that same QE-modernization playbook to HHAeXchange's platform, bringing proven experience building and scaling QA organizations from scratch across multiple product lines.

jobs.title: QA Manager
jobs.notes: Manages internal and external QA teams at Hexworks studio (Lords of the Fallen). Covers FQA, CQA, LQA. Partners with QA Managers across CI Games. Requires 2+ yrs lead QA and 5+ yrs games QA.

fit.why: Remote AAA games QA Manager with people-management scope, but seniority bar and QA Manager title are well below your Director-level background.
fit.blurb: Building quality processes at Hexworks — a studio with AAA ambitions on the Lords of the Fallen franchise — would draw on my experience standing up QA disciplines from scratch at multiple game studios. I'd bring senior-level FQA/LQA/CQA coverage, cross-studio coordination depth from Amazon Games and Fortis, and AI-driven automation strategy to help Hexworks scale quality.

jobs.title: QA Manager, Software Engineering
jobs.notes: Leads Quality Analysts supporting global development teams. Partners with Professional Services/external delivery. Burbank CA is commutable from OC. Games engine company.

fit.why: Unity's engine QA organization is games-adjacent and Burbank is commutable from OC — but QA Manager is below your Director trajectory.
fit.blurb: Leading QA for Unity's software engineering teams would let me apply 20 years of game-development quality leadership to the engine layer that powers the entire industry — from global CI/CD strategy to modern automation frameworks. I'd bring director-level rigor and my current AI/agentic-testing focus to help Unity's QA team raise quality velocity across Professional Services delivery.

jobs.title: QA Manager
jobs.notes: Leads QA Engineers + manual testers on AAA HD title in Bucharest expansion studio. Taylor's former employer. Under-leveled vs Director scope held at Amazon; requires international relocation to Romania.

fit.why: Amazon Games is Taylor's former employer but returning as QA Manager is a step down from the Director/Head scope held there, and Bucharest requires international relocation.
fit.blurb: Amazon Games is where I built my AAA QA leadership — overseeing New World, Lost Ark, and Crucible across global teams. While I know this organization well, re-entering at QA Manager level would be a step back from the Director scope I've held since, and the Bucharest location requires relocating abroad — I'd watch for a Director-level opening in a US Amazon Games studio.

jobs.title: Director, QA
jobs.notes: Oversees all QA operations at the Bloomington/Minneapolis MN office across multiple concurrent AAA projects. Primary QA contact for all studio teams. 12+ yrs games QA experience and 4+ yrs leading large-scale departments required. Call of Duty franchise.

fit.why: Director owning all QA operations for a major AAA studio — your exact scope at Amazon Games/Fortis — though onsite Minneapolis requires a cross-country move.
fit.blurb: Overseeing all QA efforts across multiple concurrent titles at a studio of Activision's scale mirrors exactly what I led at Amazon Games — coordinating New World, Crucible, and Lost Ark across global teams — and what I do today at Fortis, where I also lead an AI-driven transformation targeting an 80% cut in manual release-test time. I bring 12+ years of video game industry experience at Director level, ISTQB CTAL-TM certification, and the large-scale department leadership this Minneapolis role demands.

jobs.title: Software Quality Assurance (QA) Director
jobs.notes: Leads globally dispersed QA team for Benevity's CSR/ESG SaaS platform. Defines QA strategy and roadmap, owns test automation strategy and AI-driven testing approaches, builds and mentors QA organization. 10+ yrs Agile/remote QA experience required.

fit.why: Remote SaaS QA Director with explicit AI-driven testing mandate maps directly to your Fortis AI transformation work — strongest non-games tech fit this scan.
fit.blurb: Defining QA strategy and championing AI-driven testing approaches for a globally dispersed team is the work I lead at Fortis today — using LLMs and agentic AI to target an 80% reduction in manual release testing across a remote-first organization. I'd bring the same quality-first engineering culture and automation modernization playbook to Benevity's SaaS platform, with 20 years of experience building and scaling QA disciplines from scratch.

jobs.title: Director of Quality Engineering
jobs.notes: Leads and oversees QA strategy, processes, and team for ClickUp's SaaS productivity platform. Implements robust testing frameworks and drives automation initiatives. Visionary QE leadership role.

fit.why: Director-level QE leadership at a high-growth SaaS company; automation focus aligns well even with the games-to-SaaS domain shift.
fit.blurb: Building and driving QA strategy across a complex SaaS product — implementing robust testing frameworks and pushing automation maturity — is a direct translation of my CI/CD and test-automation leadership at Fortis, Amazon Games, and Blizzard. I'd bring director-level rigor for scaling QA organizations and modernizing automation infrastructure, including my current AI-driven testing initiative targeting an 80% reduction in manual release work.

jobs.title: Director of Quality Assurance
jobs.notes: QA Director for LenderAI — an AI-native SaaS platform processing $11B+ in SBA/non-SBA loans. Leads QA Community of Practice. $160K-$250K comp. Fort Lauderdale FL (hybrid). Small-mid size fintech with AI product at core.

fit.why: QA leadership for an AI-native SaaS product triggers the AI bonus — testing LLM-driven platform outputs is Taylor's emerging specialty — offsetting the fintech domain shift.
fit.blurb: Delivering quality assurance for an AI-native platform — where test strategy must account for non-deterministic LLM outputs and agentic workflows — is exactly the challenge I'm solving at Fortis today, where I lead an AI transformation targeting an 80% reduction in manual release testing. My experience building QA disciplines from scratch and owning quality strategy across complex product suites would let me apply that AI-testing expertise directly to LenderAI.

jobs.title: Head of QA
jobs.notes: Leads QA organization for DriveWealth's global B2B fintech API trading platform. Oversees functional and automation QA engineers. Hybrid New York. Prioritizes quality, resiliency, and innovation across the engineering org.

fit.why: Head of QA title is right-leveled but fintech domain shift plus hybrid NYC office makes this a meaningful stretch from your games/CA base.
fit.blurb: Leading a QA organization that prioritizes quality, resiliency, and innovation across high-throughput API infrastructure mirrors my live-service incident management and CI/CD discipline at Blizzard, Amazon, and Fortis — where real-time reliability is non-negotiable at scale. I'd apply that proven framework for building high-performing QA teams and automation architecture to DriveWealth's trading platform.

jobs.title: Senior Quality Assurance Director – Dead by Daylight
jobs.notes: Senior Director-level QA role for Dead by Daylight (60M+ players), Canada's largest independent game studio. Hybrid Montreal. QA Team Leads report up through this role. Live-service asymmetric multiplayer flagship.

fit.why: Senior Director owning QA for Dead by Daylight's live-service multiplayer at 60M+ players — excellent scope match — though hybrid Montreal requires international relocation to Canada.
fit.blurb: Leading quality for Dead by Daylight — a live-service multiplayer title at the scale I've built toward across Fortis, Amazon Games, and Blizzard — aligns precisely with my Director-level live-ops QA experience. I'd bring an automation-first quality culture and 20+ years of multiplayer platform QA to help Behaviour Interactive keep pace with one of the world's largest asymmetric multiplayer communities.

jobs.title: QA Manager (Project Orion)
jobs.notes: Building the QA team from scratch for Project Orion (Cyberpunk 2 sequel) at CDPR's Boston Hub. Owns QA strategy, process, team-building, and vendor alignment. Application closes 09/01/2026. Onsite Boston.

fit.why: Building QA from scratch for a Cyberpunk AAA title is meaningful ownership, but QA Manager is well below your Director trajectory and requires onsite relocation to Boston.
fit.blurb: Standing up QA processes and a team from the ground floor for Project Orion at CD Projekt Red is the kind of greenfield build I've done repeatedly — at Fortis, Amazon Games, and Netflix — and CDPR's AAA ambitions match the caliber of work I care about. That said, the QA Manager title is a step back from my Director scope, and the onsite Boston requirement would mean a cross-country relocation from Orange County.
